deviations.description: The new requirement obligates doctor approval before discharge for patients over 65 or with more than 5 medications, but the process only includes a nurse approval for patients over 65 and does not address medication count or doctor approval. The process fails to meet the stricter requirement.

The new requirement extends the precondition to include patients with more than 5 medications, but the process does not evaluate or branch on medication count, resulting in missing required approvals for this group.
The process model only implements nurse approval for discharge and does not include any activity for doctor approval, violating the new requirement that mandates doctor approval.

The change in the age threshold from over 65 to 65 is a minor adjustment, but the process does not implement a check for age 65 specifically; it only checks for under 65, so the deviation is already captured by the broader non-compliance.
The deletion of the nurse resource constraint has no impact because the process already only allows nurse approval, which is now insufficient under the new requirement.
The deletion of the 24-hour time constraint for approval does not introduce non-compliance, but the process still only allows nurse approval, which is insufficient under the new requirement.
The extension of the temporal validity period does not affect compliance, as the process is already non-compliant with the stricter requirements.
